Luis, Analog is a meta-framework based on Angular, which among other things aims to
improve the developer experience by adding a layer of conventions, of tools for
the creation of web applications with hybrid rendering techniques, both on the client side
as well as the server side. And that's what we'll be talking about today. But before that, I'd like to

in this space and contributing to us with your knowledge about this meta-framework.
No, thank you for the invitation. Thanks. Yes, you are originally from Mérida, Yucatán.
Currently you reside a bit further north in Tampa, Florida. You are a mechatronics graduate.
You have over a decade of professional experience in technical leadership, engineering
frontend engineering, and you are one of the contributors to the core team of Analog, the Angular framework.
So, do you remember when was the first time you heard about Analog?

differences between doing it with Angular and doing it with Analog." So,
the first thing that stands out is its orientation—not orientation, because we can't
